name: no-logo
description: Naomi Klein's No Logo — anti-corporate globalization toolkit exposing brand power, sweatshop labor, the corporate takeover of public space, and the rise of global activism fighting for democratic control of the commons
useCase: Understand the power of brands, the global supply chain, corporate expansion into public space, and the activism that fights back
knownRisks: The book was published in 1999 — some specific examples are dated (though the analysis remains relevant)
Klein's perspective is explicitly anti-corporate — not all readers will agree with her conclusions
Some activists have criticized the book for overstating the effectiveness of consumer campaigns
